message.abstract:
<jats:title>Abstract</jats:title>
                <jats:p>As the first wave of COVID-19 recedes, policymakers are contemplating the relaxation of shelter-in-place orders. Using a model capturing high-risk populations and transmission rates estimated from hospitalization data, we find that postponing relaxation will only delay a second wave and cocooning vulnerable populations is needed to prevent overwhelming medical surges.</jats:p>
title: Cocooning is essential to relaxing social distancing
